Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine in blood and urine, indicating significant renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine color/appearance, supporting renal dysfunction and possible tubular damage. HbA1c is low, suggesting no chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection. Findings are consistent with acute or chronic kidney disease with nephrotic-range proteinuria; urgent nephrology evaluation is recommended.
